definition: China's monetary/fiscal policy mix may be the single most important driver of base metals outcomes. China holds ~55% of global copper consumption and is rotating from construction-heavy steel consumption (-2-3%/yr) toward copper-intensive applications (+4-6%/yr via grid investment, EVs, data centers).
evidence: Rotation from construction steel (-2-3%/yr) to copper-intensive grid/EV/AI (+4-6%/yr)
Supported by customs data and industry sources
China credit impulse independently flagged by fx_strategist_02 as underweighted EM factor
open_questions: Needs broader corroboration for single most important framing
How does China's property sector downturn offset copper-intensive investment?
What are the implications for copper supply adequacy at scale?
